---
title: LMU Open Science Center Tutorial Examples
format: tutorial-template-html+base
---


::: {.callout-important}

There are essentially no differences between the `osc-brand-html` and `tutorial-template-html` templates, at least very few if any that you will notice on this page alone. The `tutorial-template-html` is just slightly more opinonated with some presets e.g. a pre-activated Table of Contents (TOC) and some code tools/overflow options.

:::

## brand.yml extension

This example shows a few Bootstrap components that inherit the palette defined in `brand.yml` as well as a customized SCSS file.

### Color badges

<span class="badge text-bg-primary">Primary</span>
<span class="badge text-bg-secondary">Secondary</span>
<span class="badge text-bg-success">Success</span>
<span class="badge text-bg-info">Info</span>
<span class="badge text-bg-warning">Warning</span>
<span class="badge text-bg-danger">Danger</span>

### Alerts

::: {.alert .alert-primary}
This is a primary alert using the brand's primary color.
:::

::: {.alert .alert-secondary}
This is a secondary alert using the brand's secondary color.
:::

::: {.alert .alert-success}
This is a success alert using the brand's success color.
:::

::: {.alert .alert-info}
This is an info alert using the brand's info color.
:::

### Buttons

<div class="d-flex flex-wrap gap-2">
  <button type="button" class="btn btn-primary">Primary action</button>
  <button type="button" class="btn btn-secondary">Secondary action</button>
  <button type="button" class="btn btn-success">Success</button>
  <button type="button" class="btn btn-info">Info</button>
  <button type="button" class="btn btn-warning">Warning</button>
  <button type="button" class="btn btn-danger">Danger</button>
</div>

### Progress

<div class="progress my-3" role="progressbar" aria-label="Brand progress" aria-valuenow="68" aria-valuemin="0" aria-valuemax="100" style="height: 1.25rem;">
  <div class="progress-bar bg-primary" style="width: 68%">Primary 68%</div>
</div>

<div class="progress my-3" role="progressbar" aria-label="Secondary progress" aria-valuenow="42" aria-valuemin="0" aria-valuemax="100" style="height: 1.25rem;">
  <div class="progress-bar bg-secondary" style="width: 42%">Secondary 42%</div>
</div>

<div class="progress my-3" role="progressbar" aria-label="Info progress" aria-valuenow="24" aria-valuemin="0" aria-valuemax="100" style="height: 1.25rem;">
  <div class="progress-bar bg-info" style="width: 24%">Info 24%</div>
</div>

### Cards

```{=html}
<div class="row row-cols-1 row-cols-md-3 g-3">
  <div class="col">
    <div class="card border-primary h-100">
      <div class="card-header text-bg-primary">Primary</div>
      <div class="card-body">
        <h5 class="card-title">Brand anchor</h5>
        <p class="card-text">Use the primary color for the main action or strongest emphasis.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="col">
    <div class="card border-secondary h-100">
      <div class="card-header text-bg-secondary">Secondary</div>
      <div class="card-body">
        <h5 class="card-title">Supporting state</h5>
        <p class="card-text">The secondary color is useful for quieter supporting UI and neutral emphasis.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="col">
    <div class="card border-info h-100">
      <div class="card-header text-bg-info">Info</div>
      <div class="card-body">
        <h5 class="card-title">Informational state</h5>
        <p class="card-text">Use the info color for helpful notes, tips, and contextual guidance.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="col">
    <div class="card border-danger h-100">
      <div class="card-header text-bg-danger">Danger</div>
      <div class="card-body">
        <h5 class="card-title">Attention state</h5>
        <p class="card-text">Reserve the danger color for destructive or error-related feedback.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### Inline code and links

Here is some inline code: `def foo(x): return x`{.python} and `y`{.python} and `z`{.python}.

Here is [a link](https://example.com).

### Code block

```{.r}
# | echo: true
fibonacci <- function(n) {
  if (n <= 0) {
    return(NULL)
  } else if (n == 1) {
    return(0)
  } else if (n == 2) {
    return(1)
  } else {
    return(fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2))
  }
}
```

### Images

Brand images can be accessed with the `{{</* brand logo NAME */>}}` shortcode. Available options include:

* **Icon, default, dark**: these are the underlying image labels. However, shoull use the superset of these:
* **Small, medium, large**: small maps to the icon (or favicon), and medium and large are currently set to use the General Logo (and to swap the colors accordingly with light/dark mode--this is the main motivator to use these labels instead of the other ones!)

#### Favicon

```markdown
{{</* brand logo small */>}}
```

{{< brand logo small >}}

#### General Logo

##### Medium

```markdown
{{</* brand logo medium */>}}
```

{{< brand logo medium >}}

Note that if you switch to dark mode, the "LMU Open Science Center" text switches to white. This functions not because the actual text color is changing, but because a different image is swapped in. That is, the the `medium` logo has a light mode and dark mode image defined.

##### Large

The large logo is essentially the same, but without a dark mode option available at this time.

```markdown
{{</* brand logo large */>}}
```

{{< brand logo large >}}
